ucs.title: [LLM] Gorgon Group: Office macro → hidden-window scripting host → process hollowing into RegAsm/MSBuild (.NET LOLBin)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Gorgon Group (Subaat) infection chain reported by Unit 42 in 2018 and observed continuously since: a weaponized Office document drops a VBScript/PowerShell stub which downloads a commodity RAT (NJRat, RevengeRAT, LokiBot, Remcos, AZORult) and process-hollows it into a signed .NET utility — most often RegAsm.exe, RegSvcs.exe, MSBuild.exe, InstallUtil.exe or aspnet_compiler.exe. Catches the chain by joining Stage 1 (Office → hidden script host with download/IEX/base64 markers) to Stage 2 (script host spawning a .NET LOLBin, the hollow target) within a 5-minute window on the s
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as stage1_time count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe") AND (Processes.process="*hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-w 1*" OR Processes.process="*-windowstyle*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-Expression*" OR Processes.process="*IEX*" OR Processes.process="*-EncodedCommand*" OR Processes.process="*pastebin*" OR Processes.process="*bit.ly*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename parent_process_name as office_parent, process_name as script_host, process as script_cmd | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as hollow_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("regasm.exe","regsvcs.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe","aspnet_compiler.exe","caspol.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process_name as hollow_target, process as hollow_cmd, parent_process_name as hollow_parent ] | where hollow_time >= stage1_time AND (hollow_time - stage1_time) <= 300 | table dest user stage1_time hollow_time office_parent script_host script_cmd hollow_target hollow_parent hollow_cmd
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 5m;
let OfficeApps     = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let ScriptHosts    = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let HollowTargets  = dynamic(["regasm.exe","regsvcs.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe","aspnet_compiler.exe","caspol.exe"]);
let Stage1 = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeApps)
    | where FileName in~ (ScriptHosts)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("hidden","-w 1","-windowstyle","FromBase64String","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-Expression"," iex ","-EncodedCommand","pastebin","bit.ly","j.mp")
    | project Stage1Time = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              OfficeParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ScriptHost   = FileName,
              ScriptCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
              ScriptPid    = ProcessId;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName in~ (HollowTargets)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptHosts)
| join kind=inner Stage1 on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (Stage1Time .. Stage1Time + WindowMin)
| project DeviceName, AccountName, Stage1Time, HollowTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec    = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, Stage1Time),
          OfficeParent, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd,
          HollowParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          HollowTarget = FileName,
          HollowCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          HollowSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Stage1Time desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 5m;
let OfficeApps    = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let ScriptHosts   = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let HollowTargets = dynamic(["regasm.exe","regsvcs.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe","aspnet_compiler.exe","caspol.exe"]);
let Stage1 = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend ParentFile = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1])),
             ChildFile  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    @"\")[-1]))
    | where ParentFile in (OfficeApps)
    | where ChildFile  in (ScriptHosts)
    | where CommandLine has_any ("hidden","-w 1","-windowstyle","FromBase64String","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-Expression"," iex ","-EncodedCommand","pastebin","bit.ly","j.mp")
    | project Stage1Time = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              OfficeParent = ParentFile, ScriptHost = ChildFile,
              ScriptCmd    = CommandLine, ScriptPid = NewProcessId;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ChildFile  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    @"\")[-1])),
         ParentFile = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ChildFile  in (HollowTargets)
| where ParentFile in (ScriptHosts)
| join kind=inner Stage1 on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (Stage1Time .. Stage1Time + WindowMin)
| project Computer, Account, Stage1Time, HollowTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, Stage1Time),
          OfficeParent, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd,
          HollowTarget = ChildFile, HollowCmd = CommandLine
| order by Stage1Time desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Gorgon Group .NET LOLBin Hollow-Target Spawned by Script Host
id: 9b1c4d8e-7a2f-4b6e-9c11-7d3a4e5f6c20
status: experimental
description: Detects the second stage of the Gorgon Group infection chain — a .NET utility (RegAsm, RegSvcs, MSBuild, InstallUtil, aspnet_compiler) spawned by a Windows script host (wscript/cscript/powershell/mshta) used as a process-hollowing target for NJRat/Remcos/LokiBot/AZORult.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0078/
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/unit42-aggah-campaign-bit-ly-blogspot-and-pastebin-used-for-c2-in-large-scale-campaign/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1055.012
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1564.003
  - attack.g0078
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\RegAsm.exe'
      - '\RegSvcs.exe'
      - '\MSBuild.exe'
      - '\InstallUtil.exe'
      - '\aspnet_compiler.exe'
      - '\caspol.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Developer build automation invoking MSBuild from a script wrapper
  - Custom in-house deployment tooling that scripts InstallUtil/RegAsm
level: high
ucs.rationale: This isn't a generic 'Office spawns PowerShell' template — it specifically requires the THREE-stage Gorgon signature: (1) Office parent, (2) hidden-window script host with downloader / base64 / Bitly-Pastebin markers (Aggah campaign infrastructure), and (3) a .NET LOLBin spawned by that same script host within 5 minutes on the same device. RegAsm/MSBuild process hollowing is Gorgon's near-exclusiv

ucs.title: [LLM] Gorgon Group: Run-key persistence pointing to script-host payload in user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts Gorgon Group's persistence step: after the initial PowerShell/VBScript stager executes, the actor writes an HKCU\...\Run or RunOnce value whose data invokes wscript/cscript/powershell/mshta against a payload staged in %APPDATA%\Roaming, %TEMP%, %ProgramData% or %Public% — exactly the location set used by the Aggah/Subaat operators. Filtering on script-host + user-writable directory in the value data eliminates almost all legitimate vendor installers, which use signed EXE paths under Program Files.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Explorer\\Shell Folders\\Startup*") AND Registry.action IN ("created","modified") AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*wscript*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*cscript*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*powershell*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*pwsh*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*mshta*") AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Roaming\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Temp\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Public\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*") by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data Registry.process_name Registry.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | where NOT (process_name IN ("msiexec.exe","explorer.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","squirrel.exe","update.exe")) | sort 0 - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["wscript","cscript","powershell","pwsh","mshta"]);
let UserWritable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Roaming\", @"\Local\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Explorer\Shell Folders\Startup", @"\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run")
| where RegistryValueData has_any (ScriptHosts)
| where RegistryValueData has_any (UserWritable)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","squirrel.exe","update.exe")
| extend SuspiciousScriptArg = iif(RegistryValueData has_any (".vbs",".vbe",".js",".jse",".ps1",".hta","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","-w 1","hidden"), true, false)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          User = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
          SuspiciousScriptArg,
          WriterProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          WriterCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          WriterParent  = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ScriptHosts  = dynamic(["wscript","cscript","powershell","pwsh","mshta"]);
let UserWritable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Roaming\", @"\Local\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
ImRegistryEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Explorer\Shell Folders\Startup", @"\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run")
| where RegistryValueData has_any (ScriptHosts)
| where RegistryValueData has_any (UserWritable)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where ActingProcessName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","squirrel.exe","update.exe")
| extend SuspiciousScriptArg = iif(RegistryValueData has_any (".vbs",".vbe",".js",".jse",".ps1",".hta","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","-w 1","hidden"), true, false)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData,
          SuspiciousScriptArg, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Gorgon Group's persistence pattern is highly stylised — they almost never drop a signed EXE persistence; instead they chain HKCU\Run → wscript/powershell → script under %APPDATA%\Roaming or %ProgramData% (documented in Unit 42 'Aggah' and 'Subaat' write-ups, and replicated in 2019-2024 campaigns by Cybereason and Morphisec). Three filters make this fire only on the actor pattern: Run-family regist
